message:
security(fcp-core): use content-addressed IDs for approval tokens

SECURITY FIX: Prevent approval token malleability attacks.

Previously, approval_token_object_id() derived the ObjectId solely from
the token_id string. This created a vulnerability where an attacker
could craft a malicious token with the same token_id but different
content (e.g., different target_integrity or scope), and the policy
engine would treat it as equivalent to a legitimate token.

The fix changes approval_token_object_id() to compute the ObjectId
from the full canonical CBOR encoding of the ApprovalToken. This
ensures that any modification to the token content produces a
different ObjectId, making token substitution attacks impossible.

Key changes:
- approval_token_object_id() now serializes the full token to
  deterministic CBOR before hashing
- Falls back to token_id bytes only if CBOR serialization fails
  (defensive, should never occur in practice)
- Add unit test demonstrating that tokens with same token_id but
  different content produce different ObjectIds

The test vectors for decision_allow_execution_match and
decision_allow_with_declassification have been updated to reflect
the new content-addressed evidence hashes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
